archetype: Real-Time Cryogenic Phase-Transition Front Propagation & Boil-Off Gas (BOG) Suppression Controller for Orbital Liquid Hydrogen Depot in LEO
domain: In-Space Propellant Logistics & Cryogenic Fluid Dynamics
description: A deterministic control system that orchestrates helium pressurization and active thermal shield actuation to maintain liquid hydrogen (LH2) in a zero-gravity orbital depot, preventing propellant loss via boil-off and ensuring correct phase distribution for subsequent engine refueling. It is used by autonomous satellite tugs and ground-based orbital traffic controllers, requiring sub-millisecond latency to react to micro-vibrations and solar flare thermal spikes while managing multi-phase fluid dynamics in a vacuum.
rules: MUST implement control loops compliant with ECSS-E-ST-31C (Thermal Control) and ECSS-Q-ST-80C (On-Orbit Software) standards for spaceflight software.
MUST interface with radiation-hardened sensors via MIL-STD-1553B or SpaceWire protocols with built-in ECC error correction for telemetry.
MUST utilize non-linear Model Predictive Control (MPC) to solve Navier-Stokes equations for sloshing and phase-change boundaries in simulated zero-G environments.
MUST enforce a 'Fail-Safe' logic where any sensor disagreement triggers an immediate passive thermal venting sequence to prevent tank over-pressurization.
MUST integrate real-time solar X-ray flux data from NOAA SWPC APIs to dynamically adjust active cooling loads before thermal saturation occurs.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard REST/HTTP APIs for control loops, as network latency variances in deep-space communication (1+ seconds RTT) will cause catastrophic oscillation in cryogenic pressure regulation.

DO NOT rely on cloud-based machine learning models for immediate actuator adjustments; all critical decision logic must be hardened and onboard to handle space radiation bit-flips without external dependency.
DO NOT assume constant atmospheric pressure or gravity; standard fluid dynamics libraries that ignore surface tension dominance in micro-gravity will yield physically impossible simulation results.
key_technical_challenges: Modeling and predicting the complex interaction between ullage gas expansion and liquid hydrogen stratification in the absence of convection currents.
Implementing fault-tolerant state estimation algorithms that can distinguish between sensor failure due to cosmic ray strikes and genuine cryogenic fluid anomalies.
Optimizing the energy budget for active cryocoolers against the severe constraints of solar power availability during eclipse orbits.

recommended_stack.language: Rust (with no_std support) for memory safety and deterministic execution in a resource-constrained, radiation-hardened environment.
recommended_stack.framework: OrcV2 (Onboard Real-time Control) or a custom FreesRTOS port with zero-dynamic memory allocation guarantees.
recommended_stack.database_engine: SQLite with Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) configured for flash-memory endurance and atomic transaction recovery against power cycles.
recommended_stack.message_broker: DDS (Data Distribution Service) with Real-Time Publish-Subscribe profile for low-latency sensor-to-actuator communication.
special_services: Radiation Event Counter and State Machine

compliance_flags: NASA NPR 8705.4 (Risk Classification for NASA Missions)
